summary: The Malta Business Registry (MBR) holds the statutory register and issues a certified company extract and a certificate of registration. Under Companies Act art.424 any person may inspect documents kept by the Registrar and obtain a certified copy or extract. Core identifying data (name, number, legal form, registered office, incorporation date, status, share capital, officers) is publicly available; the extract is the functional equivalent of a German Registerauszug.

evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain a copy of the certificate of the registration of any commercial partnership or a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.
evidence.note: General public-access provision covering all Registrar-held documents.

evidence.quote: A certificate of registration given in respect of a company is conclusive evidence that the requirements of this Act in respect of registration and of matters precedent and incidental to it have been complied with and that the company is duly registered as a public or private company under this Act.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal (official) — Malta business register
evidence.quote: company names and registration numbers, their registered address, date of incorporation, whether it is a company or not, share capital, and the identity of the company officials.
evidence.note: Free public information tier; further documents available for a charge.

caveats: INFERENCE: Malta has no statutory document literally called a 'Registerauszug'; the MBR-issued company extract / certificate of registration is the functional equivalent.
The free online extract is a data snapshot; a certified extract must be ordered and carries a fee.

summary: Identical basis to the Ltd: art.424 makes all Registrar-held documents inspectable and obtainable as certified copies/extracts, and art.77(2) makes the certificate of registration conclusive evidence of due registration as a public company. The MBR issues a company extract that functions as the register extract.

evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain a copy of the certificate of the registration of any commercial partnership or a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

A certificate of registration given in respect of a company is conclusive evidence that the requirements of this Act in respect of registration and of matters precedent and incidental to it have been complied with and that the company is duly registered as a public or private company under this Act.

caveats: INFERENCE: same mapping caveat as the Ltd — 'Registerauszug' is our label for the MBR company extract.

documentLocal: annual return (Seventh Schedule, Part 3 — List of Past and Present Members)
summary: CONFIRMED: Malta's annual return IS a full shareholder list and IS public. Art.184 requires every company, on each anniversary of registration, to file an annual return in the form of the Seventh Schedule. Part 3 is the 'List of Past and Present Members' giving each member's name, address and number of shares held. It is a document kept by the Registrar and thus inspectable/obtainable by any person under art.424. The founding shareholders are additionally named as subscribers in the memorandum (art.69). A separately-ordered document, not part of the base extract, hence 'additional'.

evidence.quote: Every company shall, after 1st January 2004, upon each anniversary of its registration, make a return in the form set out in the Seventh Schedule showing the matters therein specified and made up to the date of such anniversary

3. List of Past and Present Members. List of persons holding shares or stock in the company on the date to which this return is made up, and of persons who have held shares or stock therein at any time since the date of the last return, or in the case of the first return, of the registration of the company.
evidence.note: Columns: Names / Addresses / Number of shares held by existing members.

evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain ... a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal (official) — documents obtainable from MBR
evidence.quote: This documentation includes, inter alia, all the notifications submitted and registered by the Registrar for every company, the status of the company, annual accounts as well as annual returns.

caveats: STALENESS: the annual return is filed only once a year (within 42 days of the registration anniversary), so at any given moment it may be up to ~13 months out of date and will not reflect share transfers since the last filing.

The company-held register of members (art.123) is the live record but is kept at the registered office and open to inspection only by members without charge (art.125) — NOT a Registrar/MBR document. The public MBR proof is the annual return.

Do not confuse with the beneficial-ownership register: BO is a separate filing whose public access was curtailed after the CJEU Nov-2022 ruling; the annual-return member list is unaffected and remains public.

documentLocal: annual return (Seventh Schedule, Part 3 — List of Past and Present Members)
summary: The art.184 annual-return obligation and the Seventh Schedule Part 3 'List of Past and Present Members' apply to ALL companies, with no exemption for public companies (the only carve-out from Parts 2 and 3 is for investment companies with variable share capital / SICAVs, art.84(8)). So a plc's annual return also legally lists every member and their shareholding, and it is public under art.424. Founding subscribers are additionally in the memorandum (art.69).

evidence.quote: Every company shall, after 1st January 2004, upon each anniversary of its registration, make a return in the form set out in the Seventh Schedule showing the matters therein specified and made up to the date of such anniversary

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 84(8) — scope of exemption (only SICAVs excused)
evidence.quote: an investment company with variable share capital shall not be obliged to give any of the details in Parts 2 and 3 of the form of annual return set out in the Seventh Schedule, other than the share capital of the company and the number of shares issued.
evidence.note: Confirms a plain plc has NO such exemption — it must list members.

evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ...

caveats: STALENESS: same annual, up-to-~13-months lag as the Ltd — the list IS complete but is a yearly snapshot.

NOMINEE/LISTED RISK: for a plc whose shares are admitted to trading, the registered members shown in the annual return are frequently nominees, custodians or a CSD, not the ultimate beneficial holders, and registered holdings turn over continuously; the document proves the register of members, not beneficial ownership.
BO-register public-access restriction (post-CJEU 2022) does not affect the annual-return member list.

summary: A Maltese company's constitution is its memorandum of association (art.69) plus its articles of association (art.75). Both are delivered to and registered by the Registrar (art.76) and held as the company's 'statute' at the MBR; any person may obtain a certified copy under art.424. Treated as an additional (separately-ordered) document rather than the base extract.

evidence.quote: The memorandum of every company shall state: (a) whether the company is a public company or a private company; (b) the name and residence of each of the subscribers thereto; (c) the name of the company; (d) the registered office in Malta ...; (e) the objects of the company; (f) the amount of share capital ...

There may be registered with the memorandum, articles of association (hereinafter referred to as "articles") which shall be signed by the subscribers to the memorandum and prescribing regulations for the company.
evidence.note: If no articles are registered, the model articles in the First Schedule apply (art.75(2)).

evidence.quote: The memorandum and articles, if any, shall be delivered for registration to the Registrar who, being satisfied that all the requirements ... have been complied with, shall register them.

caveats: A private company may register bespoke articles OR rely on the First Schedule model articles (art.75(2)).
The MBR-held copy reflects the memorandum and articles as last amended and registered; unregistered/pending amendments would not appear.

summary: Same constitutional framework as the Ltd: memorandum (art.69, stating it is a public company) and articles (art.75), both registered with the Registrar (art.76) and obtainable as certified copies by any person (art.424). A public company must additionally annex a formation-costs/special-advantages statement (art.69(2)), likewise a registered document.

evidence.quote: The memorandum of every company shall state: (a) whether the company is a public company or a private company ... (2) In the case of a public company, there shall be annexed to the memorandum a document providing: (a) the total amount or an estimate of all the costs payable by the company or chargeable to it by reason of its formation ... and (b) a description of any special advantage granted prior to the time the company is authorized to commence business ...
The memorandum and articles, if any, shall be delivered for registration to the Registrar who, being satisfied that all the requirements ... have been complied with, shall register them.
Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain ... a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

caveats: The MBR copy reflects the constitution as last registered; any unregistered amendment would not be evidenced.
Model-articles fallback (art.75(2)) applies as for any company where articles are not separately registered.
